theme: Small talk đầu giờ & pantry chat

vocab.example: Are you planning any vacation this summer?
vocab.example_vi: Bạn có định đi nghỉ hè không?

vocab.example: Do you want to grab lunch break together today?
vocab.example_vi: Bạn có muốn ăn trưa cùng nhau hôm nay không?

vocab.example: What do you do as a hobby outside of work?
vocab.example_vi: Ngoài công việc, sở thích của bạn là gì?

vocab.example: I just moved to a new neighborhood last month.
vocab.example_vi: Tôi vừa chuyển đến khu phố mới tháng trước.

vocab.example: I go to the gym before work three times a week.
vocab.example_vi: Tôi đi tập gym trước giờ làm ba lần một tuần.

vocab.example: The weather has been really hot this week.
vocab.example_vi: Thời tiết tuần này thực sự rất nóng.

vocab.example: I like reading books in my spare time.
vocab.example_vi: Tôi thích đọc sách trong thời gian rảnh.

vocab.example: My colleague recommended a great restaurant nearby.
vocab.example_vi: Đồng nghiệp của tôi gợi ý một nhà hàng ngon gần đây.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: Hey Tuan, do you want to grab lunch break together today?

lines.speaker: Tuan
lines.text: Sure! Actually, my colleague recommended a new place nearby.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: Nice, let's try it. By the way, what do you do in your spare time?

lines.speaker: Tuan
lines.text: I usually go to the gym, and reading is also a hobby of mine.

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: That's great. I just moved to a new neighborhood, so I'm still exploring.

lines.speaker: Tuan
lines.text: Oh nice, how do you like it so far?

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: It's quiet, but the weather has been really hot this week.

lines.speaker: Tuan
lines.text: I know, terrible. Are you planning any vacation this summer?

lines.speaker: Hoa
lines.text: Yes, I'm thinking about visiting Da Nang for a few days.

questions.q: Who recommended the new lunch place?
What does Tuan do in his spare time?
options: Going to the gym and reading

questions.q: Where is Hoa planning to go for vacation?

shadowing: Do you want to grab lunch break together today with the team?
What do you usually do in your spare time after work?
I just moved to a new neighborhood, so I'm still exploring the area.
The weather has been really hot this week, hasn't it?
Are you planning any vacation this summer, maybe somewhere near the beach?

interview.question: What do you like to do in your spare time?
tips_vi: Trả lời ngắn gọn, chân thật, có thể liên hệ nhẹ đến kỹ năng làm việc
Tránh liệt kê quá nhiều, chọn 1-2 sở thích chính
Có thể kết bằng lý do bạn thích hoạt động đó
interview.sample_answer: In my spare time, I enjoy going to the gym about three times a week — it helps me stay energetic and reduce stress after a long day at work. I also like reading books, especially about technology and personal growth, and I usually finish one or two books a month. On weekends, I sometimes explore new neighborhoods or try new restaurants with friends, which helps me relax and disconnect from work completely. I recently picked up cooking too, since I moved into a new apartment with a bigger kitchen. I think having hobbies outside of work helps me stay balanced and actually makes me more focused and productive when I'm working, because I don't feel burned out.

interview.question: How important is small talk with colleagues to you?
tips_vi: Nêu quan điểm cá nhân kèm lý do cụ thể
Nhấn mạnh vai trò xây dựng mối quan hệ và tinh thần đội nhóm
Có thể đưa ví dụ ngắn từ kinh nghiệm thực tế
interview.sample_answer: I think small talk is quite important, because it helps build a friendly atmosphere at work, especially on a team with people from different backgrounds. For example, I like chatting with colleagues during lunch break about simple things, like the weather, weekend plans, or a new restaurant nearby. It makes people feel more comfortable with each other, so when we later need to discuss a difficult work topic, like a missed deadline, communication is already much easier and less stressful. I believe good relationships between colleagues can really improve teamwork in the long run, because people are more willing to help each other and admit mistakes early. In my current team, this kind of small talk has honestly made our daily standups feel more relaxed and productive.
